title: Optimization of CV-QKD Under Practical Constraints
authors: Svitlana Matsenko, Amirhossein Ghazisaeidi, Marcin Jarzyna, Konrad Banaszek, Darko Zibar
abstract: Using reinforcement learning, we optimize for practical hardware constraints, including limited FIR filter taps at the transmitter and receiver, mean photon number and finite DAC/ADC resolution. Under these realistic conditions, the proposed approach achieves significant performance improvements.
summary: This paper uses reinforcement learning to optimize the hardware parameters of a CV-QKD system. By jointly tuning filters and signal intensity, the researchers achieved much higher key rates and longer transmission distances despite realistic hardware limitations like low-resolution converters.
main_problem: Optimizing the performance of Continuous-Variable Quantum Key Distribution (CV-QKD) under practical hardware constraints such as finite filter lengths, limited DAC/ADC resolution, and inter-symbol interference.
main_result: The joint optimization of transmitter/receiver filters and mean photon number significantly improves the Secure Key Rate (SKR) and extends the maximum transmission distance from 60 km to nearly 100 km.
method: An end-to-end reinforcement learning approach using the REINCOFFE policy gradient algorithm to update filter weights and photon number without requiring gradient computation.
model_or_system: A CV-QKD system over optical fiber networks using Gaussian-modulated symbols, coherent detection, and standard telecommunication components including DAC/ADC and FIR filters.
important_parameters: Filter lengths (L_TX, L_RX), DAC/ADC bit resolution (b), transmission distance (L), channel excess noise (n_ch), and mean photon number (n*)
main_assumptions: Quantization errors from DAC and ADC are modeled as equivalent Gaussian additive noise terms.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the system architecture; Fig 2 shows SKR vs resolution and filter amplitude response; Fig 3 shows the gap to maximum SKR relative to hardware resolution and filter length; Fig 4 shows SKR vs distance for different noise levels.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the hardware constraints in CV-QKD, describes the reinforcement learning optimization framework, presents results regarding performance gains and distance extension, and analyzes the efficiency of hardware requirements.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
